annotations.label: Quantum field theory

anchor.snippet: quantum field theory ( QFT ) is a theoretical framework that combines field theory

annotations.note: QFT as a theoretical framework is a physics concept with no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classical field

anchor.snippet: A classical field is a function of spatial and time coordinates.

annotations.note: No notion of a classical physical field is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real scalar field

anchor.snippet: The simplest classical field is a real scalar field

annotations.note: A real scalar field in the physics sense is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The simplest classical field is a real scalar field

annotations.label: Klein–Gordon equation

anchor.snippet: This is known as the Klein–Gordon equation

annotations.note: A grep for Klein–Gordon finds nothing; this relativistic wave equation is not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: This is known as the Klein–Gordon equation

annotations.label: Normal mode decomposition

anchor.snippet: so its solutions can be expressed as a sum of normal modes

annotations.note: The normal-mode (Fourier) decomposition of Klein–Gordon solutions is not formalized as such.

anchors.snippet: so its solutions can be expressed as a sum of normal modes

annotations.label: Creation and annihilation operators

anchor.snippet: are replaced by the annihilation operator

annotations.note: Creation/annihilation operators (CCR/CAR algebras) are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: are replaced by the annihilation operator

annotations.label: Vacuum state

anchor.snippet: which is the lowest energy state, is defined by

annotations.note: The QFT vacuum (lowest-energy) state is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: which is the lowest energy state, is defined by

annotations.label: Fock space

anchor.snippet: The latter space is known as a Fock space

annotations.note: A grep for Fock space returns nothing; Fock space is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Second quantization

anchor.snippet: is often also called second quantization

annotations.note: Second quantization is a physics procedure with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Quartic interaction term

anchor.snippet: a quartic interaction term could be introduced to the Lagrangian of the real scalar field

annotations.note: Interaction Lagrangians for scalar fields are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: a quartic interaction term could be introduced to the Lagrangian of the real scalar field

annotations.label: Feynman path integral

anchor.snippet: the above product of integrals becomes the Feynman path integral

annotations.note: The Feynman path integral is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the above product of integrals becomes the Feynman path integral

annotations.label: Two-point correlation function

anchor.snippet: and goes by multiple names, like the two-point propagator

annotations.note: The QFT two-point correlation function/propagator is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: and goes by multiple names, like the two-point propagator

annotations.label: Feynman propagator

anchor.snippet: The free two-point function, also known as the Feynman propagator

annotations.note: The Feynman propagator is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The free two-point function, also known as the Feynman propagator

annotations.label: Wick's theorem

anchor.snippet: Wick's theorem further reduce any

annotations.note: The only "Wick" matches are an author's name; Wick's/Isserlis' theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Wick's theorem further reduce any

annotations.label: Feynman rules

anchor.snippet: the so-called Feynman rules

annotations.note: Feynman rules and diagrams are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Connected diagrams

anchor.snippet: Connected diagrams are those in which every vertex is connected to an external point through lines

annotations.note: Although graph connectivity exists, connected Feynman diagrams as a QFT concept are not formalized.

anchors.snippet: Connected diagrams are those in which every vertex is connected to an external point through lines

annotations.label: Tree-level and n-loop diagrams

anchor.snippet: Feynman diagrams devoid of "loops" are called tree-level diagrams

annotations.note: Tree-level and loop Feynman diagrams are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regularization

anchor.snippet: This is an example of regularization

annotations.note: Regularization of divergent integrals in QFT is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Renormalized perturbation theory

anchor.snippet: A different approach, called renormalized perturbation theory

annotations.note: Renormalized perturbation theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A different approach, called renormalized perturbation theory

annotations.label: Renormalizable theories

anchor.snippet: It is only possible to eliminate all infinities to obtain a finite result in renormalizable theories

annotations.note: Renormalizability of field theories is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Renormalization group

anchor.snippet: is a mathematical apparatus used to study the changes in physical parameters

annotations.note: The renormalization group is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Beta function

anchor.snippet: The way in which each parameter changes with scale is described by its β function

annotations.note: Mathlib's beta function is the Euler integral, unrelated to the renormalization-group β function, which is not formalized.

annotations.label: Callan–Symanzik equation

anchor.snippet: change with scale according to the Callan–Symanzik equation

annotations.note: The Callan–Symanzik equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: QED beta function

anchor.snippet: namely the elementary charge e , has the following β function

annotations.note: The QED coupling β function is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: namely the elementary charge e , has the following β function

annotations.label: Asymptotic freedom (QCD)

anchor.snippet: a phenomenon known as asymptotic freedom

annotations.note: Asymptotic freedom in QCD is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: a phenomenon known as asymptotic freedom

annotations.label: Conformal field theories

anchor.snippet: Conformal field theories (CFTs) are special QFTs that admit conformal symmetry

annotations.note: Mathlib has conformal maps but not conformal field theories as QFTs.

annotations.label: QED Lagrangian

anchor.snippet: quantum electrodynamics contains a Dirac field ψ representing the electron field

annotations.note: The QED Lagrangian with Dirac and photon fields is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: quantum electrodynamics contains a Dirac field ψ representing the electron field

annotations.label: Tree-level QED diagram

anchor.snippet: Shown above is an example of a tree-level Feynman diagram in QED

annotations.note: QED Feynman diagrams are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauge symmetry

anchor.snippet: then the transformation is referred to as a gauge symmetry of the theory

annotations.note: The concept of gauge symmetry in field theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: then the transformation is referred to as a gauge symmetry of the theory

annotations.label: U(1) gauge symmetry of QED

anchor.snippet: thus QED is said to have U(1) gauge symmetry

annotations.note: The gauge group U(1) exists as `Circle` (and `Matrix.unitaryGroup`), but the gauge symmetry of QED itself is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: thus QED is said to have U(1) gauge symmetry

annotations.label: SU(3) gauge symmetry of QCD

anchor.snippet: is a non-Abelian gauge theory with an SU(3) gauge symmetry

annotations.note: The gauge group SU(3) exists as `Matrix.specialUnitaryGroup`, but QCD as a gauge theory with this symmetry is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: is a non-Abelian gauge theory with an SU(3) gauge symmetry

annotations.label: Anomaly

anchor.snippet: a phenomenon called anomaly

annotations.note: Quantum anomalies are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noether's theorem

anchor.snippet: Noether's theorem states that every continuous symmetry

annotations.note: Mathlib has Noetherian rings but not the physics Noether's theorem relating symmetries to conservation laws.

annotations.label: Spontaneous symmetry breaking

anchor.snippet: Spontaneous symmetry breaking is a mechanism whereby the symmetry of the Lagrangian is violated by the system described by it

annotations.note: Spontaneous symmetry breaking is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear sigma model

anchor.snippet: consider a linear sigma model containing N real scalar fields

annotations.note: The linear sigma model is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: consider a linear sigma model containing N real scalar fields

annotations.label: Goldstone's theorem

anchor.snippet: Goldstone's theorem states that under spontaneous symmetry breaking

annotations.note: Goldstone's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldstone boson equivalence theorem

anchor.snippet: The Goldstone boson equivalence theorem states that at high energy

annotations.note: The Goldstone boson equivalence theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higgs mechanism

anchor.snippet: a process called the Higgs mechanism

annotations.note: The Higgs mechanism is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supersymmetry

anchor.snippet: a type of symmetry, called supersymmetry , that relates bosons and fermions

annotations.note: Supersymmetry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Super-Poincaré group

anchor.snippet: is known as the super-Poincaré group

annotations.note: The super-Poincaré group is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supergravity

anchor.snippet: then the resultant gauge theory is an extension of general relativity called supergravity

annotations.note: Supergravity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: QFT in curved spacetime

anchor.snippet: For QFTs in curved spacetime on the other hand, a general metric

annotations.note: No Lorentzian/curved-spacetime QFT is formalized; Mathlib's "Minkowski" refers to inequalities and geometry of numbers.

anchors.snippet: For QFTs in curved spacetime on the other hand, a general metric

annotations.label: Topological quantum field theory

anchor.snippet: For a special class of QFTs called topological quantum field theories (TQFTs), all correlation functions are independent of continuous changes in the spacetime metric

annotations.note: Topological quantum field theories are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chern–Simons theory

anchor.snippet: Chern–Simons theory is an example of TQFT

annotations.note: Chern–Simons theory is not formalized in Mathlib (the only "Chern" matches are Chernoff bounds).

annotations.label: Non-perturbative methods

anchor.snippet: non-perturbative methods in QFT treat the interacting Lagrangian as a whole without any series expansion

annotations.note: Non-perturbative QFT methods are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Haag's theorem

anchor.snippet: according to Haag's theorem , there does not exist a well-defined interaction picture for QFT

annotations.note: Haag's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constructive quantum field theory

anchor.snippet: This line of study is called constructive quantum field theory

annotations.note: Constructive quantum field theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic quantum field theory

anchor.snippet: Algebraic quantum field theory is another approach to the axiomatization of QFT

annotations.note: Algebraic quantum field theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wightman and Haag–Kastler axioms

anchor.snippet: Axiomatic systems following this approach include Wightman axioms and Haag–Kastler axioms

annotations.note: The Wightman and Haag–Kastler axioms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Osterwalder–Schrader axioms

anchor.snippet: which give the necessary and sufficient conditions for a real time theory to be obtained from an imaginary time theory

annotations.note: The Osterwalder–Schrader axioms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Yang–Mills existence and mass gap

anchor.snippet: Yang–Mills existence and mass gap , one of the Millennium Prize Problems

annotations.note: The Yang–Mills existence and mass gap problem is not formalized in Mathlib.
